title: Ask for Directions
summary: Ask a local person or staff member for clear directions when lost or unsure.
learnerPromise: You will be able to confidently ask a stranger or staff member for directions and understand their response well enough to follow it.
whyItMatters: Even with phones and maps, there are times when asking a person is the fastest or most reliable way to find your destination. It also helps you practice clear communication in unfamiliar situations.
realLifeUses: Asking a store clerk how to find a specific building nearby
Checking with a transit worker to confirm which platform your train leaves from
Asking a passerby for help when your phone battery is low or signal is weak
Requesting directions to a restroom or exit in a large public building
result: You receive clear directions you can repeat back and follow.
youWillLearn: How to choose the right person to ask for help
How to phrase your question clearly and politely
How to listen for landmarks and turn instructions
How to repeat the directions back to confirm you understood
miniChallenge: Ask a staff member, shop worker, or friendly passerby for directions to a nearby place you already know. Repeat the directions back to them to confirm you understood.
steps: Pick someone who looks approachable, such as a staff member, shop worker, or person not in a rush.
Greet them politely and say the name of the place you are trying to find.
Ask a specific question like 'Is it within walking distance?' or 'Which direction should I go?'
Listen carefully and note any landmarks, street names, or turn instructions they mention.
Repeat the key directions back to them to make sure you got them right.
completionCriteria: You asked a clear, polite question about directions
You repeated the directions back and confirmed they were correct
commonProblems: The person may not know the location or may give confusing directions
You might feel nervous or unsure about approaching a stranger
Directions given quickly can be hard to remember all at once
tips: Look for people wearing uniforms, name tags, or standing near information desks
If you did not catch something, politely ask them to repeat it
Write down or mentally pair each turn with a landmark to make it easier to remember
